name: Inverted-F Antenna (microstrip)
description: A compact planar antenna consisting of a horizontal radiating element connected to a ground plane through a shorting pin, with a separate feed point. The inverted-F configuration provides good impedance matching and compact size, making it ideal for mobile communications. The antenna exhibits monopole-like radiation characteristics with linear polarization.

length.name: Radiating Element Length
length.description: Length of the horizontal radiating element, typically quarter wavelength

width.name: Radiating Element Width
width.description: Width of the horizontal radiating element affecting bandwidth

height.name: Antenna Height
height.description: Height above ground plane, affects impedance and bandwidth

shortPosition.name: Short Pin Position
shortPosition.description: Distance from antenna end to shorting pin, affects resonant frequency

feedPosition.name: Feed Point Position
feedPosition.description: Distance from shorting pin to feed point, controls input impedance

groundLength.name: Ground Plane Length
groundLength.description: Length of ground plane, affects radiation pattern

groundWidth.name: Ground Plane Width
groundWidth.description: Width of ground plane, affects radiation pattern

designMethodology.overview: Design process involves determining antenna dimensions for desired frequency, optimizing feed and short positions for impedance matching, and adjusting geometry for bandwidth requirements.

steps.title: Calculate Initial Length
steps.description: Determine radiating element length for fundamental resonance

steps.title: Set Antenna Height
steps.description: Choose height based on bandwidth and size constraints

steps.title: Position Shorting Pin
steps.description: Place shorting pin to achieve desired resonant frequency

steps.title: Optimize Feed Position
steps.description: Adjust feed point location for 50-ohm impedance matching

steps.title: Design Ground Plane
steps.description: Size ground plane for desired radiation characteristics

keyAssumptions: Thin wire approximation for radiating element
Substrate losses neglected in first-order analysis

references: Balanis, C.A., 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Wiley, 2016
Stutzman, W.L. and Thiele, G.A., 'Antenna Theory and Design', 3rd Edition, Wiley, 2012
Volakis, J.L., 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, McGraw-Hill, 2007
